title: Email notifications arriving hours late
body: Task assignment notifications used to arrive within a minute or two. For the past week, they've been arriving 2-4 hours late. This is causing people to miss deadlines because they don't know they've been assigned a task until hours later. It seems to be worse in the morning — notifications sent around 9-10am take the longest to arrive. Afternoon notifications are usually faster but still delayed.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for the detailed report — the morning timing pattern is a really useful clue.

Do you know if anything changed about a week ago when this started? For example:

- **A) A deployment or code change** to TaskFlow or its notification system
- **B) A change to your email infrastructure** (mail provider, DNS, relay config)
- **C) An increase in usage** (more users, more task assignments than usual)
- **D) Nothing obvious changed** — it just started happening
- **E) Something else**

This will help narrow down whether we're looking at a queue bottleneck, a misconfiguration, or a third-party issue.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Now that you mention it, I think there was a new feature rolled out around that time — some kind of daily digest or summary email? I started getting a marketing-ish digest email in the mornings, I want to say it started showing up right around when the delays began. I'm not sure if that's related or just a coincidence. I don't know about any infrastructure changes though. Option E I guess — the digest email thing is the only change I noticed.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
That's a really strong lead — thank you. Here's what I think is happening:

The new daily digest emails are likely sent as a batch job in the morning, and they probably go through the **same email sending queue** as your task assignment notifications. When that batch fires (likely around 8–9am), it floods the queue with digest emails, and your transactional notifications get stuck behind them. That would explain why morning notifications are delayed the most (they're competing with the digest batch) and afternoon ones are faster (the digest backlog has cleared by then).

Does that match what you're seeing — specifically, do the digest emails themselves arrive in the morning around the time the notification delays are worst?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Yeah, that sounds about right. The digest email usually hits my inbox somewhere around 9am, and that's exactly when the delays are the worst. By the afternoon things seem to mostly catch up. I hadn't connected the two but now that you lay it out like that, the timing lines up pretty perfectly. Is there a way to fix this without killing the digest feature entirely? Some people on my team actually like getting the summary.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Task assignment email notifications delayed 2-4 hours due to queue contention with daily digest batch job","problem":"Task assignment notifications that previously arrived within 1-2 minutes are now delayed 2-4 hours. The delays began approximately one week ago, coinciding with the rollout of a new daily digest/summary email feature. Delays are worst in the morning (9-10am) when the digest batch fires, and improve in the afternoon as the queue drains.","root_cause_hypothesis":"The daily digest batch job sends a large volume of emails through the same queue as transactional task-assignment notifications. When the digest batch fires in the morning, it floods the queue, and transactional notifications are processed FIFO behind the digest emails rather than being prioritized. This creates a multi-hour backlog that clears by afternoon.","reproduction_steps":["Wait for the daily digest batch to fire (likely around 8-9am)","Assign a task to a user shortly after the digest batch starts","Observe that the assignment notification email is delayed by 2-4 hours","Assign a task in the afternoon (after digest backlog clears) and observe faster delivery"],"environment":"Production environment. Affects all users receiving task assignment notifications. Timing correlates with daily digest batch schedule.","severity":"high","impact":"All users receiving task assignment notifications are affected. People are missing deadlines because they don't learn about new assignments for hours. Morning assignments are most impacted.","recommended_fix":"Separate transactional emails (task assignments, deadline reminders) from bulk/batch emails (daily digests) so they don't compete for the same queue. Options include: (1) Use a dedicated high-priority queue for transactional notifications, (2) Use a separate email sending pipeline for batch digests, (3) Rate-limit the digest batch job so it doesn't saturate the shared queue, (4) Send digests via a different email service or provider entirely. Option 1 or 2 is preferred as it structurally prevents recurrence.","proposed_test_case":"Enqueue a batch of N digest emails (matching production volume), then immediately enqueue a transactional notification. Verify the transactional notification is delivered within the expected SLA (e.g., under 2 minutes) regardless of digest queue depth.","information_gaps":["Exact queue implementation (Redis, SQS, database-backed, etc.) — developer can determine from codebase","Number of digest emails sent per batch — developer can check from logs or batch job config","Whether the email provider has sending rate limits contributing to the backlog"]}
